# fly_secret (Resource)

Manages a secret for a Fly.io application. Import using app_name/SECRET_KEY: `terraform import fly_secret.example my-app/DATABASE_URL`.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "fly_app" "example" {
  name     = "my-secret-app"
  org_slug = "personal"
}

resource "fly_secret" "database_url" {
  app   = fly_app.example.name
  key   = "DATABASE_URL"
  value = "postgres://user:pass@host:5432/db"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

> **NOTE**: [Write-only arguments](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments) are supported in Terraform 1.11 and later.

- `app` (String) The name of the Fly.io application. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.
- `key` (String) The secret key name. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.
- `value` (String, Sensitive, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) The secret value. The API never returns this value. Write-only: not persisted in state.

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (String) Timestamp when the secret was created.
- `digest` (String) SHA256 hex digest of the secret value, used for drift detection.
- `id` (String) Synthetic identifier in the format app/key.
